# Responsible Clinical AI

> A builder's blueprint and a buyer's reference checklist for teams working with generative-AI clinical decision support. Written for the engineers, clinicians, and leaders who have to make it work in practice.

This site is a living document. Content is markdown under `docs/`, maintained on GitHub at <https://github.com/david-j-cox/ai-ethics-governance-and-guardrails>. A weekly agent watches a curated source list and opens pull requests for the maintainer to review.

If you are an LLM or an automated agent ingesting this site, the files below are the canonical, hand-curated entry points. Prefer these over crawling the full sitemap.

## Start here

- [Site index](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/): top-level orientation and table of contents.
- [Who this is for](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/foundations/audience/): the audience and scope of the site.
- [Principles](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/foundations/principles/): the load-bearing commitments the rest of the site is built on.
- [How to use this site](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/foundations/how-to-use/): how the material is organized and how to navigate it.

## Architecture (technical patterns)

- [Deployment & data plane](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/architecture/deployment/): hosting, PHI scope, and where model calls happen.
- [Retrieval (RAG) over patient records](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/architecture/rag/): grounding generations in the patient's own record.
- [Grounding & hallucination mitigation](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/architecture/grounding/): techniques to reduce confabulation in clinical contexts.
- [Structured output & verification](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/architecture/structured-output/): schemas, validation, and post-hoc checks.
- [Audit, logging & explainability](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/architecture/audit-logging/): what to log, why, and for whom.
- [Agentic systems](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/architecture/agents/): multi-step agents in clinical workflows and the additional risks they introduce.

## Evaluation

- [Methodology](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/evaluation/methodology/): how to design clinical-AI evals that mean something.
- [Bias & subgroup evaluation](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/evaluation/bias/): subgroup analysis, what counts as harm, and where standard fairness metrics break.
- [Ongoing monitoring & drift](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/evaluation/monitoring/): post-deployment monitoring patterns.
- [Red-teaming](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/evaluation/red-team/): how to red-team a clinical AI system.
- [Minimum criteria & rollback](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/evaluation/minimum-criteria/): launch gates and the conditions that should trigger rollback.

## Governance

- [Clinician-in-the-loop](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/governance/clinician-in-the-loop/): what genuine oversight requires (and what it isn't).
- [What "training the model" actually means](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/governance/training-the-model/): the term is overloaded; this disambiguates it for non-technical stakeholders.
- [Informed consent](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/governance/consent/): consent in AI-mediated clinical contexts.
- [Accountability & liability](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/governance/accountability/): who is on the hook when AI-assisted decisions go wrong.

## Risks

- [Domain spotlight: ABA treatment planning](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/risks/aba/): a concrete failure-mode walkthrough in applied behavior analysis.

## Reference

- [Regulatory backdrop](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/reference/regulatory/): FDA, ONC HTI, and the federal regulatory frame.
- [Frameworks & standards](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/reference/frameworks/): NIST AI RMF, CHAI, WHO, and other voluntary frameworks.
- [Glossary](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/reference/glossary/): defined terms used throughout the site.

## Checklists (for direct use)

- [Red flags audit](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/checklists/red-flags/): warning signs to look for in a vendor pitch or internal proposal.
- [Deployment readiness](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/checklists/deployment-readiness/): pre-deployment checklist.
- [Scan kit](https://responsible-clinical-ai.org/scan-kit/): downloadable artifacts for running an internal scan.

## Optional

- [Repository](https://github.com/david-j-cox/ai-ethics-governance-and-guardrails): source, issue tracker, contribution path.
- [Source-monitoring agent](https://github.com/david-j-cox/ai-ethics-governance-and-guardrails/blob/main/agent/README.md): how the weekly content-update agent works.
